verses.sanskrit:
ॐ उशन् ह वै वाजश्रवसः सर्ववेदसं ददौ ।
तस्य ह नचिकेता नाम पुत्र आस ॥
verses.english:
Yearning ( superior   rewards,)   Vajasravasa   gifted   all   his
material   wealth.   He   had   a   son   named   Nachiketa.

verses.sanskrit:
तँ ह कुमारँ सन्तं दक्षिणासु
नीयमानासु श्रद्धाविवेश सोऽमन्यत ॥
verses.english:
On   the   gifts  being   fetched  for  giving , Nachiketa,  though
a   young   lad,   was   overcome   with   religious   fervour   and he  pondered   :

verses.sanskrit:
पीतोदका जग्धतृणा दुग्धदोहा निरिन्द्रियाः ।
अनन्दा नाम ते लोकास्तान् स गच्छति ता ददत् ॥
verses.english: Joyless,   indeed,   are   the   worlds   to   which   he   goes   who   gives as   gifts   such   old   cows   that   cannot   calve  nor  produce   milk any more. They had also drank water for the last time and eaten grass terminally.  (Giving   away   useless   articles   as   gift   in   a   sacrifice is   a   sin   and   cannot   bring   good   reward.)

verses.sanskrit:
स होवाच पितरं तत कस्मै मां दास्यसीति ।
द्वितीयं तृतीयं तँ होवाच मृत्यवे त्वा ददामीति ॥
verses.english:
He   addressed   his   father   :   "Father,   to   whom  will  you give  me away?"
He   repeatedly  said   so,  twice  and   thrice. Upon  this  the   father  replied   :   "I   shall   give   you   to   Death"

verses.sanskrit:
बहूनामेमि प्रथमो  बहूनामेमि मध्यमः ।
किँ स्विद्यमस्य कर्तव्यं यन्मयाऽद्य करिष्यति ॥
verses.english:
(Nachiketa   thought   within   himself)   :   Among   many   of
(my   father's  disciples),   I   am   either   at   the   top   or   middle
(but   never   at the  bottom ).   What   then   will   he   achieve
in   giving   me   to   Yama ?

verses.sanskrit:
अनुपश्य यथा पूर्वे प्रतिपश्य तथाऽपरे ।
सस्यमिव मर्त्यः पच्यते सस्यमिवाजायते पुनः ॥
verses.english: Remember   how   your   forefathers   behaved .   Consider   how   men   of   worth conduct   themselves   today.   Like   vegetation   man   ripens   and decays,   and   like   vegetation  he   is   born   again .

verses.sanskrit:
वैश्वानरः प्रविशत्यतिथिर्ब्राह्मणो गृहान् ।
तस्यैताँ शान्तिं कुर्वन्ति हर वैवस्वतोदकम् ॥
verses.english: (Nachiketa goes to  the   abode   of   Yama,   but   the   latter   was   not at   home.  Nachiketa had   to wait   for   three   days   without   food. On   Yama's return, he   is   told   by   his   people):  A  Brahmana   guest   entering a house-hold resembles fire. He is offered appeasement. O ! Vaivasvata (Yama),   fetch   water (for washing the feet and sipping).

verses.sanskrit:
आशाप्रतीक्षे संगतँ सूनृतां
    चेष्टापूर्ते पुत्रपशूँश्च सर्वान् ।
एतद्वृङ्क्ते पुरुषस्याल्पमेधसो
    यस्यानश्नन्वसति ब्राह्मणो गृहे ॥
verses.english: Hopes   and   expectations,   the   benefits   of   association   with   the good, the   merit   arising from   sweet   and   beneficial   speech, the deeds prescribed in the scriptures and moral codes , children   and   cattle,   all   these   are   destroyed   in   the case   of   the person of dull intellect in whose house a holy guest has remained unattended.

verses.sanskrit:
तिस्रो रात्रीर्यदवात्सीर्गृहे मे-
    ऽनश्नन् ब्रह्मन्नतिथिर्नमस्यः ।
नमस्तेऽस्तु ब्रह्मन् स्वस्ति मेऽस्तु
    तस्मात्प्रति त्रीन्वरान्वृणीष्व ॥
verses.english: Yama   said   to   Nachiketa  : O   holy   and   venerable   guest,   since   you   have   dwelt   in   my   house   for   three nights   without   food,   choose   three   boons   from   me. I bow   to   you;   may all   be   well   with   me.

verses.sanskrit:
शान्तसंकल्पः सुमना यथा स्याद्
    वीतमन्युर्गौतमो माऽभि मृत्यो ।
त्वत्प्रसृष्टं माऽभिवदेत्प्रतीत
    एतत् त्रयाणां प्रथमं वरं वृणे ॥
verses.english: (Nachiketa   thereupon   said) - O   Death,  may  I  have the   first   of   the   three   boons to (my   father)    Gautama   being   in happy frame of mind   and   of  peaceful disposition anger;   and   may he  recognise me   and   receive   me  when I   am   released  by   you.

verses.sanskrit:
यथा पुरस्ताद् भविता प्रतीत
    औद्दालकिरारुणिर्मत्प्रसृष्टः ।
सुखँ रात्रीः शयिता वीतमन्यु
    स्त्वां ददृशिवान्मृत्युमुखात् प्रमुक्तम् ॥
verses.english: (Yama   said   :)  By   my   order,   Auddalaki   Aruni   (your   father)  will sleep peacefully at night. He will recognise   you and   treat   you   as   in the past,   having   seen you  released   from   the   jaws   of   death.  And he will be without   anger . (The   mental   agony   caused   to   Nachiketa's   father   has   been   removed  by the   first   boon).

verses.sanskrit:
स्वर्गे लोके न भयं किंचनास्ति
    न तत्र त्वं न जरया बिभेति ।
उभे तीर्त्वाऽशनायापिपासे
    शोकातिगो मोदते स्वर्गलोके ॥
verses.english: (Nachiketa   said:)  In   heaven   there   is   no   fear   at all ;   death   and   old   age   are   not there.   Having   overcome   hunger   and   thirst   and   all   sorrow,   one   rejoices in   the   heavenly   world.

verses.sanskrit:
स त्वमग्निँ स्वर्ग्यमध्येषि मृत्यो
    प्रब्रूहि त्वँ श्रद्दधानाय मह्यम् ।
स्वर्गलोका अमृतत्वं भजन्त
    एतद् द्वितीयेन वृणे वरेण ॥
verses.english: O   Death, You  know  the   Fire   (sacrifice)   that   leads   to   heaven. Instruct  me on that  who   am   full   of   faith .   Those   in   heaven   are   deathless. This   I   choose   as   my   second   boon.

verses.sanskrit:
प्र ते ब्रवीमि तदु मे निबोध
    स्वर्ग्यमग्निं नचिकेतः प्रजानन् ।
अनन्तलोकाप्तिमथो प्रतिष्ठां
    विद्धि त्वमेतं निहितं गुहायाम् ॥
verses.english: O   Nachiketa,  I  know   about   the   Fire   that   leads   to   heaven. This I  shall impart   to   you .  Listen to me carefully.   Know  this to be the   means  of  attaining   eternal   heaven   and   also   the   support   of   the   world.   This knowledge   is   hidden   in   the   cave of the heart.

verses.sanskrit:
लोकादिमग्निं तमुवाच तस्मै
    या इष्टका यावतीर्वा यथा वा ।
स चापि तत्प्रत्यवदद्यथोक्त
    मथास्य मृत्युः पुनरेवाह तुष्टः ॥
verses.english: Yama   then   explained   that   Fire   (sacrifice),  is the   origin of   the   world.   He   gave   details   of   what   type   of   bricks   and   how   many are   required   (for   the   altar)   and   how   the   fire   is   to   be   lit.   And Nachiketa   repeated   all   the   instructions   given   to   him.   Then   Yama, being   pleased   at   this,   said   again.

verses.sanskrit:
तमब्रवीत् प्रीयमाणो महात्मा
    वरं तवेहाद्य ददामि भूयः ।
तवैव नाम्ना भविताऽयमग्निः
    सृङ्कां चेमामनेकरूपां गृहाण ॥
verses.english: That high-souled (Yama) happily, said   to Nachiketa "I  shall  here and now give you another  boon:  may  this   fire   be   named after you; and may you  also accept this   multi-coloured garland.

verses.sanskrit:
त्रिणाचिकेतस्त्रिभिरेत्य सन्धिं
    त्रिकर्मकृत्तरति जन्ममृत्यू ।
ब्रह्मजज्ञं देवमीड्यं विदित्वा
    निचाय्येमाँ शान्तिमत्यन्तमेति ॥
verses.english: He  transcends cyclic existence who has perfomed the Nachiketa fire-sacrifice three times and has received instructions from the three (mother,father   and   preceptor), and has also discharged   his   three-fold  duties   (study   of   Vedas,   performance   of   Sacrifice and   giving   alms), having realised the all pervading shining one worthy of worship-the fire god born of Brahman. Such a person also attains supreme peace.

verses.sanskrit:
त्रिणाचिकेतस्त्रयमेतद्विदित्वा
    य एवं विद्वाँश्चिनुते नाचिकेतम् ।
स मृत्युपाशान् पुरतः प्रणोद्य
    शोकातिगो मोदते स्वर्गलोके ॥
verses.english: The man of wisdom, having known the injunctions performs the Nachiketa sacrifice three times. Thus annhilating the noose of death even before the fall of his body, crosses the (ocean of ) grief and enjoys in heaven.

verses.sanskrit:
एष तेऽग्निर्नचिकेतः स्वर्ग्यो
    यमवृणीथा द्वितीयेन वरेण ।
एतमग्निं तवैव प्रवक्ष्यन्ति जनासः
    तृतीयं वरं नचिकेतो वृणीष्व ॥
verses.english: This   is   your   fire,  which  takes one   to   heaven   and   which you   have   chosen   as   your   second   boon. People   will   designate this   fire   after your   name. Now   choose   your   third boon, O Nachiketa.

verses.sanskrit:
येयं प्रेते विचिकित्सा मनुष्ये-
    ऽस्तीत्येके नायमस्तीति चैके ।
एतद्विद्यामनुशिष्टस्त्वयाऽहं
    वराणामेष वरस्तृतीयः ॥
verses.english: (Nachiketa   said): "Upon the death of  a person, there arises this doubt - some declare that something outlives the body, other say, nothing remains. I desire to know, upon your instructions, about this issue and this is my third among the boons"

verses.sanskrit:
देवैरत्रापि विचिकित्सितं पुरा
    न हि सुविज्ञेयमणुरेष धर्मः ।
अन्यं वरं नचिकेतो वृणीष्व
    मा मोपरोत्सीरति मा सृजैनम् ॥
verses.english:
(Yama   said) : " This subtle matter is not easily understood. In the days of yore, even
Gods had entairtained doubts about this. Therefore, O! Nachiketa! choose
another boon. Do not press this and release me from this (Obligation)"

verses.sanskrit:
देवैरत्रापि विचिकित्सितं किल
    त्वं च मृत्यो यन्न सुज्ञेयमात्थ ।
वक्ता चास्य त्वादृगन्यो न लभ्यो
    नान्यो वरस्तुल्य एतस्य कश्चित् ॥
verses.english: (Nachiketa   said) : " O! Death! you too say that even the gods had indeed doubted about this and is not also easy to comprehend. There is neither a teacher equal to you nor a boon equal to this".

verses.sanskrit:
शतायुषः पुत्रपौत्रान्वृणीष्वा
    बहून्पशून् हस्तिहिरण्यमश्वान् ।
भूमेर्महदायतनं वृणीष्व
    स्वयं च जीव शरदो यावदिच्छसि ॥
verses.english: (Yama   said) : "May you seek sons and grandsons who will live a hundred years, cattle, elephants, horses and gold, as also an extensive land area to rule and live any number of autumns as you like.

verses.sanskrit:
एतत्तुल्यं यदि मन्यसे वरं
    वृणीष्व वित्तं चिरजीविकां च ।
महाभूमौ नचिकेतस्त्वमेधि
    कामानां त्वा कामभाजं करोमि ॥
verses.english: If you think so, seek another boon equal to this for riches, longivity and an expansive kingdom where O! Nachiketa ! May you rule over. I shall make you the enjoyer of all your desires.

verses.sanskrit:
ये ये कामा दुर्लभा मर्त्यलोके
    सर्वान् कामाँश्छन्दतः प्रार्थयस्व ।
इमा रामाः सरथाः सतूर्या
    न हीदृशा लम्भनीया मनुष्यैः ।
आभिर्मत्प्रत्ताभिः परिचारयस्व
    नचिकेतो मरणं माऽनुप्राक्षीः ॥
verses.english: Whatever   desires   are  there which are not easy to be gained in this world of mortals, all those may you obtain as you desire. These beautiful damsels along with chariots and musical instruments, which are not attainable by men, I shall bestow upon you. Entertain yourself with these. Do not seek to know about death.

verses.sanskrit:
श्वोभावा मर्त्यस्य यदन्तकैतत्
    सर्वेंद्रियाणां जरयन्ति तेजः ।
अपि सर्वं जीवितमल्पमेव
    तवैव वाहास्तव नृत्यगीते ॥
verses.english: "O! Death! All these are impermanent. The sense organs of man is worn out through indulgence in these enjoyments. The entire life is but brief. You may keep these vehicles, music and dance to yourself.

verses.sanskrit:
न वित्तेन तर्पणीयो मनुष्यो
    लप्स्यामहे वित्तमद्राक्ष्म चेत्त्वा ।
जीविष्यामो यावदीशिष्यसि त्वं
    वरस्तु मे वरणीयः स एव ॥
verses.english: No man is ever satisfied with wealth. Having seen you, we shall live as long as you rule. The boon I desire is , therefore , the one I have sought.

verses.sanskrit:
अजीर्यताममृतानामुपेत्य
    जीर्यन्मर्त्यः क्वधःस्थः प्रजानन् ।
अभिध्यायन् वर्णरतिप्रमोदान्
    अतिदीर्घे जीविते को रमेत ॥
verses.english: Once the immortal and everlasting one is attained, which man, who is himself nor permanent, will be elated or be thought of living long after examining the worthless enjoyments of song & dance ?

verses.sanskrit:
यस्मिन्निदं विचिकित्सन्ति मृत्यो
    यत्साम्पराये महति ब्रूहि नस्तत् ।
योऽयं वरो गूढमनुप्रविष्टो
    नान्यं तस्मान्नचिकेता वृणीते ॥
verses.english: O! Death! Instruct me on the superior life after death regarding which people have reservations. Nachiketa chooses not other boon other than this mysterious one.

verses.sanskrit:
अन्यच्छ्रेयोऽन्यदुतैव प्रेय-
    स्ते उभे नानार्थे पुरुषँ सिनीतः ।
तयोः श्रेय आददानस्य साधु
    भवति हीयतेऽर्थाद्य उ प्रेयो वृणीते ॥
verses.english: Two concepts bind the man and are of different objectives. One is what is beneficial and the other is what is pleasing. He who chooses the beneficial, gains and the one who goes for the the pleasant, deflects from that.

verses.sanskrit:
श्रेयश्च प्रेयश्च मनुष्यमेतः
    तौ सम्परीत्य विविनक्ति धीरः ।
श्रेयो हि धीरोऽभि प्रेयसो वृणीते
    प्रेयो मन्दो योगक्षेमाद्वृणीते ॥
verses.english: Both   these - beneficial and pleasant - come to humans. The discriminating one after due consideration  selects the beneficial alone in preference to the pleasure-giving; but desirous of worldly happiness, the dull-witted falls for pleasing.

verses.sanskrit:
स त्वं प्रियान्प्रियरूपांश्च कामान्
    अभिध्यायन्नचिकेतोऽत्यस्राक्षीः ।
नैतां सृङ्कां वित्तमयीमवाप्तो
    यस्यां मज्जन्ति बहवो मनुष्याः ॥
verses.english: O! Nachiketa!,  After due consideration, you have rejected all those pleasures and objects of pleasures, which also tempt. You have not got into this path of pleasures, in which many men are lost.

verses.sanskrit:
दूरमेते विपरीते विषूची
    अविद्या या च विद्येति ज्ञाता ।
विद्याभीप्सिनं नचिकेतसं मन्ये
    न त्वा कामा बहवोऽलोलुपन्त ॥
verses.english: Widely varying with different destinations are these two- ignorance and knowledge. I think Nachiketas is desirous of knowledge as the objects of pleasure do not tempt him.

verses.sanskrit:
अविद्यायामन्तरे वर्तमानाः
    स्वयं धीराः पण्डितंमन्यमानाः ।
दन्द्रम्यमाणाः परियन्ति मूढा
    अन्धेनैव नीयमाना यथान्धाः ॥
verses.english: The deluded staying in illusion thinking themselves to be enlightened go in circles faltering and floundering like sightless-men led by a sightless one.

verses.sanskrit:
न साम्परायः प्रतिभाति बालं
    प्रमाद्यन्तं वित्तमोहेन मूढम् ।
अयं लोको नास्ति पर इति मानी
    पुनः पुनर्वशमापद्यते मे ॥
verses.english: Deluded by riches, one in illusion like a child thinks only of this world and not of a hereafter -  and thus, gets under my control repeatedly.

verses.sanskrit:
श्रवणायापि बहुभिर्यो न लभ्यः
    श‍ृण्वन्तोऽपि बहवो यं न विद्युः ।
आश्चर्यो वक्ता कुशलोऽस्य लब्धा
    आश्चर्यो ज्ञाता कुशलानुशिष्टः ॥
verses.english: It is not possible for many to hear about it (Brahman) and even having heard many do not understand. The teacher must be a skilful one and the pupil should be sharp in grasp. The knower is a wonderful person instructed by the adept.

verses.sanskrit:
न नरेणावरेण प्रोक्त एष
    सुविज्ञेयो बहुधा चिन्त्यमानः ।
अनन्यप्रोक्ते गतिरत्र नास्ति
    अणीयान् ह्यतर्क्यमणुप्रमाणात् ॥
verses.english: This principle (Brahman) is not understood by pondering in different ways or being taught by one of low ability. There is no other way than being taught by one of unparalleled ability and it transcends all arguments and is subtlest of subtle.

verses.sanskrit:
नैषा तर्केण मतिरापनेया
    प्रोक्तान्येनैव सुज्ञानाय प्रेष्ठ ।
यां त्वमापः सत्यधृतिर्बतासि
    त्वादृङ्नो भूयान्नचिकेतः प्रष्टा ॥
verses.english: This knowledge is not attainable by argumentation. It is of easy comprehension only when instructed by another. O! Dearest one! O! Nachiketa! let there be seekers like you! You are truly resolved in your desire.

verses.sanskrit:
जानाम्यहं शेवधिरित्यनित्यं
    न ह्यध्रुवैः प्राप्यते हि ध्रुवं तत् ।
ततो मया नाचिकेतश्चितोऽग्निः
    अनित्यैर्द्रव्यैः प्राप्तवानस्मि नित्यम् ॥
verses.english: I am aware that riches are not permanent. Certainly what is everlasting is not attained through what is temporary. But, I have gained the eternal by performing the nachiketa fire-sacrifice with transient materials.

verses.sanskrit:
कामस्याप्तिं जगतः प्रतिष्ठां
    क्रतोरानन्त्यमभयस्य पारम् ।
स्तोममहदुरुगायं प्रतिष्ठां दृष्ट्वा
    धृत्या धीरो नचिकेतोऽत्यस्राक्षीः ॥
verses.english: O! Nachiketa! Having seen that great and exalted state, the very foundation of the universe, the end-point of all observances, the other shore of fear, You wise one ! You have rejected the attainment of all desires, being steadfast in you resolve.

verses.sanskrit:
तं दुर्दर्शं गूढमनुप्रविष्टं
    गुहाहितं गह्वरेष्ठं पुराणम् ।
अध्यात्मयोगाधिगमेन देवं
    मत्वा धीरो हर्षशोकौ जहाति ॥
verses.english: The   wise   man   having,  by meditating on it , got that which is difficult to realise, effulgent, deep-seated and immanent, dwelling in the cave of the heart transcends   both   joy   and   sorrow.

verses.sanskrit:
एतच्छ्रुत्वा सम्परिगृह्य मर्त्यः
    प्रवृह्य धर्म्यमणुमेतमाप्य ।
स मोदते मोदनीयँ हि लब्ध्वा
    विवृतँ सद्म नचिकेतसं मन्ये ॥
verses.english: The   mortal,  having heard and realised that subtle principle, the essence of righteousness, rejoices verily, in a blissful state, having attained the bliss-worthy one. I feel that for Nachiketa , its residence is dis-covered.

verses.sanskrit:
अन्यत्र धर्मादन्यत्राधर्मा-
    दन्यत्रास्मात्कृताकृतात् ।
अन्यत्र भूताच्च भव्याच्च
    यत्तत्पश्यसि तद्वद ॥
verses.english: (Nachiketas   said): Please tell me that which you perceive as variant from the right and the wrong, the cause and effect and the past and the future.

verses.sanskrit:
सर्वे वेदा यत्पदमामनन्ति
    तपाꣳसि सर्वाणि च यद्वदन्ति ।
यदिच्छन्तो ब्रह्मचर्यं चरन्ति
    तत्ते पदꣳ सङ्ग्रहेण ब्रवीम्योमित्येतत् ॥
verses.english: That which is proclaimed by all the scriptures, which is said to be the end effect of all rituals and for which enquiry into the ultimate reality is undertaken, I shall tell you about it briefly as "OM"

verses.sanskrit:
एतद्ध्येवाक्षरं ब्रह्म एतद्ध्येवाक्षरं परम् ।
एतद्ध्येवाक्षरं ज्ञात्वा यो यदिच्छति तस्य तत् ॥
verses.english: This syllable (OM) is the highest (Brahman) , this alone is ever-lasting and whoever realises  this  attains whatever he desires.

verses.sanskrit:
एतदालम्बनँ श्रेष्ठमेतदालम्बनं परम् ।
एतदालम्बनं ज्ञात्वा ब्रह्मलोके महीयते ॥
verses.english: This (syllable OM)   is   the   best  support, this is the supreme support and upon realising this, one is adored in the world of Brahma (Hiranyagarbha loka).

verses.sanskrit:
न जायते म्रियते वा विपश्चि-
    न्नायं कुतश्चिन्न बभूव कश्चित् ।
अजो नित्यः शाश्वतोऽयं पुराणो
    न हन्यते हन्यमाने शरीरे ॥
verses.english: The consciousness (soul ) is neither born nor is it subject to death. It has not manifested from anything nor has it given rise to a new entity. This one is unborn, always existing, everlasting, indeterminable in antiquity and is not destroyed when the body (container) is destroyed.

verses.sanskrit:
हन्ता चेन्मन्यते हन्तुँ हतश्चेन्मन्यते हतम् ।
उभौ तौ न विजानीतो नायँ हन्ति न हन्यते ॥
verses.english: If   the   slayer   thinks   he  slays,   and   slain   thinks   that   he   is slain, neither   of   them   knows   the   truth.   This   (Knower)   neither slays   nor   is  slain.

verses.sanskrit:
अणोरणीयान्महतो महीया-
    नात्माऽस्य जन्तोर्निहितो गुहायाम् ।
तमक्रतुः पश्यति वीतशोको
    धातुप्रसादान्महिमानमात्मनः ॥
verses.english: Smaller   than   the   smallest,   greater   than   the   greatest   is   the   Atman that   dwells   in   the cave of the  heart   of   beings.   He   who   is   desireless,   realises the   glory   of   Atman   through   cleansing   of   mind   and   senses,   and  transcends from   sorrow.

verses.sanskrit:
आसीनो दूरं व्रजति शयानो याति सर्वतः ।
कस्तं मदामदं देवं मदन्यो ज्ञातुमर्हति ॥
verses.english: While   seated,   he   travels   far ;   lying   down   he   goes   all-over. Who   can   know   other than   me   that   Shining   One,   who  is both joyful and joyless.

verses.sanskrit:
अशरीरँ शरीरेष्वनवस्थेष्ववस्थितम् ।
महान्तं विभुमात्मानं मत्वा धीरो न शोचति ॥
verses.english: Having   realised   the   all-pervading   Supreme   Atman,   who   is   bodiless within   the   bodies,   stable   amidst   the   unstable,   the   wise one grieves no more.

verses.sanskrit:
नायमात्मा प्रवचनेन लभ्यो
    न मेधया न बहुना श्रुतेन ।
यमेवैष वृणुते तेन लभ्यः
    तस्यैष आत्मा विवृणुते तनूꣳ स्वाम् ॥
verses.english: By the study of the scriptures, lecturing one's own intellectual enquiries or by wide learning or listening, this Atman cannot be realised. Whomsoever, It chooses alone, can attain that realisation about It, as It reveals Its own nature to him.

verses.sanskrit:
नाविरतो दुश्चरितान्नाशान्तो नासमाहितः ।
नाशान्तमानसो वाऽपि प्रज्ञानेनैनमाप्नुयात् ॥
verses.english: He   who   is   not   free   from   evil   conduct,   whose   senses   are not controlled , whose   emotions   are   not   harmonised,   whose   mind   is not at   peace,   cannot   attain   this   Atman   by   any means   of   knowledge.

verses.sanskrit:
यस्य ब्रह्म च क्षत्रं च उभे भवत ओदनः ।
मृत्युर्यस्योपसेचनं क इत्था वेद यत्र सः ॥
verses.english: He   for   whom   the   Brahmana   and   the   Kshatriya   are   food, and Death   the   pickle,   who   can   know   where   He (The Self, Atman)  is   ?

verses.sanskrit:
ऋतं पिबन्तौ सुकृतस्य लोके
    गुहां प्रविष्टौ परमे परार्धे ।
छायातपौ ब्रह्मविदो वदन्ति
    पञ्चाग्नयो ये च त्रिणाचिकेताः ॥
verses.english: The cave of the heart is occupied by two entities (the cosmic self and the individual self), enjoying the fruits of their actions. Those who are realised, call them light and shade, as also the tenders of the five fires (house-holders), who had performed Nachiketa sacrifice thrice.

verses.sanskrit:
यः सेतुरीजानानामक्षरं ब्रह्म यत् परम् ।
अभयं तितीर्षतां पारं नाचिकेतँ शकेमहि ॥
verses.english: We can perform the Nachiketa sacrifice which will take us to the celestial regions, acting as a bridge. we could also attain the knowledge about the everlasting principle, Supreme Brahman, which is the place beyond the realm of fear and the refuge of the seekers of liberation - from human bondage.

verses.sanskrit:
आत्मानँ रथितं विद्धि शरीरँ रथमेव तु ।
बुद्धिं तु सारथिं विद्धि मनः प्रग्रहमेव च ॥
verses.english: The inner being is the controller (seated in) of the chariot, which is the body, the intellect the driver and the mind the reins.

verses.sanskrit:
इन्द्रियाणि हयानाहुर्विषयाँ स्तेषु गोचरान् ।
आत्मेन्द्रियमनोयुक्तं भोक्तेत्याहुर्मनीषिणः ॥
verses.english: They (the wise) say that the sense organs are the horses, the road is the sense objects and the experiencer is the body, sense organs and the mind, say the realised ones.

verses.sanskrit:
यस्त्वविज्ञानवान्भवत्ययुक्तेन मनसा सदा ।
तस्येन्द्रियाण्यवश्यानि दुष्टाश्वा इव सारथेः ॥
verses.english: That One   whose understanding of the reality is not there and whose mind is without restraint, his sense organs behave without control like the rogue horses of a charioteer.

verses.sanskrit:
यस्तु विज्ञानवान्भवति युक्तेन मनसा सदा ।
तस्येन्द्रियाणि वश्यानि सदश्वा इव सारथेः ॥
verses.english: But   he   who   has   right   understanding   and   whose   mind   is always  restrained,  his   senses   become   disciplined   like   the   obedient horses   of  a   charioteer.

verses.sanskrit:
यस्त्वविज्ञानवान्भवत्यमनस्कः सदाऽशुचिः ।
न स तत्पदमाप्नोति संसारं चाधिगच्छति ॥
verses.english: Transmigratory (cyclic) existence is for him who has no control over his mind, lacks the right understanding and is always contaminated in mind, as he never attains that goal.

verses.sanskrit:
यस्तु विज्ञानवान्भवति समनस्कः सदा शुचिः ।
स तु तत्पदमाप्नोति यस्माद्भूयो न जायते ॥
verses.english: However, that one who is discriminating , has his mind under restraint and always pure (in thought), verily he attains that destination, from where he never reverts to re-birth.

verses.sanskrit:
विज्ञानसारथिर्यस्तु मनः प्रग्रहवान्नरः ।
सोऽध्वनः पारमाप्नोति तद्विष्णोः परमं पदम् ॥
verses.english: That person who has his (discriminative) intelligence as his charioteer and whose mind has been reined in , reaches the terminus of the journey, which is that supreme abode of Lord Vishnu.

verses.sanskrit:
इन्द्रियेभ्यः परा ह्यर्था अर्थेभ्यश्च परं मनः ।
मनसस्तु परा बुद्धिर्बुद्धेरात्मा महान्परः ॥
verses.english: The   objects   of   senses   are   superior   to   the   sense organs,   the   mind is superior to the sense objects, the intellect is greater than the mind and the great Atman is   superior   to   the  intellect .

verses.sanskrit:
महतः परमव्यक्तमव्यक्तात्पुरुषः परः ।
पुरुषान्न परं किंचित्सा काष्ठा सा परा गतिः ॥
verses.english: The   Unmanifested   is   higher than the   great  ( Atman),   and   the Purusha  higher  than   the   Unmanifested.  That  is   nothing   superior to   the   Purusha,  it   is   the   acme and the supreme refuge.

verses.sanskrit:
एष सर्वेषु भूतेषु गूढोऽऽत्मा न प्रकाशते ।
दृश्यते त्वग्र्यया बुद्ध्या सूक्ष्मया सूक्ष्मदर्शिभिः ॥
verses.english: This   Atman,   is not seen, though laid  secretly  in all beings. However,  it  is  seen by   the   Seers   of   the   subtle ,   through   their   incisive and subtle power of their intellect.

verses.sanskrit:
यच्छेद्वाङ्मनसी प्राज्ञस्तद्यच्छेज्ज्ञान आत्मनि ।
ज्ञानमात्मनि महति नियच्छेत्तद्यच्छेच्छान्त आत्मनि ॥
verses.english: The   wise   should   dissolve   the   speech   in   the   mind,   the   mind in   the   intellect,   the   intellect   in   the   Great   Atman   , and   that   Great   Atman  in   the   Atman of quietitude.

verses.sanskrit:
उत्तिष्ठत जाग्रत
    प्राप्य वरान्निबोधत ।
क्षुरस्य धारा निशिता दुरत्यया
    दुर्गं पथस्तत्कवयो वदन्ति ॥
verses.english: Arise !   Awake!   Having resorted to an excellent preceptor , realise  ( the   Reality ) . The people with far-sight say that like  the sharp edge of  a razor,  that path is both difficult to set foot upon and to cross.

verses.sanskrit:
अशब्दमस्पर्शमरूपमव्ययं
    तथाऽरसं नित्यमगन्धवच्च यत् ।
अनाद्यनन्तं महतः परं ध्रुवं
    निचाय्य तन्मृत्युमुखात् प्रमुच्यते ॥
verses.english: One gets rid of the cyclic existence of births and deaths on realising that ultimate Reality, which is devoid of   sound, touch, form, perishability, taste, smell and  which is constant,  beyond the Mahat, does not undergo  any change and has  neither a beginning nor an end.

verses.sanskrit:
नाचिकेतमुपाख्यानं मृत्युप्रोक्तँ सनातनम् ।
उक्त्वा श्रुत्वा च मेधावी ब्रह्मलोके महीयते ॥
verses.english: Having listened attentively to the ancient  episode of Nachiketa,  as narrated  and  discussed  by  the  god of  Death , the Wise One  revels in the world of Brahma and  is  glorified.

verses.sanskrit:
य इमं परमं गुह्यं श्रावयेद् ब्रह्मसंसदि ।
प्रयतः श्राद्धकाले वा तदानन्त्याय कल्पते ।
तदानन्त्याय कल्पत इति ॥
verses.english: Whoever  relates with devotion ,  this highly  secret-laden  episode  in the assembly  of the learned  or  at the time of performing rites and rituals  for the  Infinitude , indeed becomes fit for the   Infinitude.

verses.sanskrit:
पराञ्चि खानि व्यतृणत् स्वयम्भू-
    स्तस्मात्पराङ्पश्यति नान्तरात्मन् ।
कश्चिद्धीरः प्रत्यगात्मानमैक्ष-
    दावृत्तचक्षुरमृतत्वमिच्छन् ॥
verses.english: The Self-Created (God)   made   the   senses   turn   outward;   hence   one looks   outward,   does   not   see   what   is   within   oneself.   Rare   is   the wise   man,   who,   longing   for   immortality,   turns   his   eyes   inward and   sees   the   inner   Atman.

verses.sanskrit:
पराचः कामाननुयन्ति बाला-
    स्ते मृत्योर्यन्ति विततस्य पाशम् ।
अथ धीरा अमृतत्वं विदित्वा
    ध्रुवमध्रुवेष्विह न प्रार्थयन्ते ॥
verses.english: The   ignorant   pursue   outward   pleasures   and   walk   into   the wide-spread   net   of   death.   The   wise,   however,  recognising  the   eternal ,    among the things that are fleeting ,  do   not   seek anything .

verses.sanskrit:
येन रूपं रसं गन्धं शब्दान् स्पर्शाꣳश्च मैथुनान् ।
एतेनैव विजानाति किमत्र परिशिष्यते । एतद्वै तत् ॥
verses.english: That   by   which   man   sees,   tastes,   smells,   hears   and   enjoys, is   the   omniscient   Lord.   By   that   alone   man   has   knowledge.   What is   there   that   remains   unknown   to   It?   This   is   indeed   That (Self that Nachiketa was asking about).

verses.sanskrit:
स्वप्नान्तं जागरितान्तं चोभौ येनानुपश्यति ।
महान्तं विभुमात्मानं मत्वा धीरो न शोचति ॥
verses.english: That   by   which   one   perceives   things   both   in   dream   and   in the waking   state -  having   known   that   as   the   great omni-present Self, the   wise   man   does   not   grieve.

verses.sanskrit:
य इमं मध्वदं वेद आत्मानं जीवमन्तिकात् ।
ईशानं भूतभव्यस्य न ततो विजुगुप्सते । एतद्वै तत् ॥
verses.english: He   who   knows the Atman, the   enjoyer   of   the   fruits   of   action, sustainer   of   life,   as   the   Lord   of   the   past   and   future,   as   close   at hand --  he   fears   no   more.     This   is   indeed   That (Self that Nachiketa was asking about).

verses.sanskrit:
यः पूर्वं तपसो जातमद्भ्यः पूर्वमजायत ।
गुहां प्रविश्य तिष्ठन्तं यो भूतेभिर्व्यपश्यत । एतद्वै तत् ॥
verses.english: He   who   sees   the   First-Born   -  born   of   knowledge,   born   before the   creation   of   waters -  sees   Him   within   the   heart,   dwelling   among the   elements ,   sees   Brahman   indeed.   This   is   verily   That (Self that Nachiketa was asking about).

verses.sanskrit:
या प्राणेन संभवत्यदितिर्देवतामयी ।
गुहां प्रविश्य तिष्ठन्तीं या भूतेभिर्व्यजायत । एतद्वै तत् ॥
verses.english: Aditi,   the   power   of   all   powers,     manifested   in   the   form   of Prana, embodied   in   the   elements,   who   resides   in   the   heart,   is   the Immortal   Self.   This   is   truly   That (Self that Nachiketa was asking about).

verses.sanskrit:
अरण्योर्निहितो जातवेदा गर्भ इव सुभृतो गर्भिणीभिः ।
दिवे दिवे ईड्यो जागृवद्भिर्हविष्मद्भिर्मनुष्येभिरग्निः । एतद्वै तत् ॥
verses.english: Like   the   child   well   guarded   in   the   womb by the pregnant women,   the   all-knowing Agni,   who   lies   hidden   in   the   fire-sticks,   is   worshipped     every day   by   the   awakened   man   and   by   those   who   offer   oblations   in  the   Fire.     This   is   verily   that (Self that Nachiketa was asking about).

verses.sanskrit:
यतश्चोदेति सूर्योऽस्तं यत्र च गच्छति ।
तं देवाः सर्वेऽर्पितास्तदु नात्येति कश्चन । एतद्वै तत् ॥
verses.english: The Atman  is really  that  from which the sun  rises  and into which  it  mixes  again.  To That (Atman)  are  all the Gods  attached  and no one  can actually go beyond it (Self or Atman that Nachiketa was asking about).

verses.sanskrit:
यदेवेह तदमुत्र यदमुत्र तदन्विह ।
मृत्योः स मृत्युमाप्नोति य इह नानेव पश्यति ॥
verses.english: Whatever is here is also there; whatever is there is the same here. He who sees a difference here travels from death to death.

verses.sanskrit:
मनसैवेदमाप्तव्यं नेह नानाऽस्ति किंचन ।
मृत्योः स मृत्युं गच्छति य इह नानेव पश्यति ॥
verses.english: By   the   purified   mind   alone   is   This   to   be   realised.   There is   no   multiplicity   here.   From   death   to   death   he   goes   ,   who   sees as   if   there   is   difference   here.

verses.sanskrit:
अङ्गुष्ठमात्रः पुरुषो मध्य आत्मनि तिष्ठति ।
ईशानं भूतभव्यस्य न ततो विजुगुप्सते । एतद्वै तत् ॥
verses.english: The   Purusha   of   the   size   of   a  thumb   dwells  within   the  body.  He is   the   lord   of   the   past   and   future.   After   knowing   Him   one   fears no   more.   This   is   verily   That (Self that Nachiketa was asking about).

verses.sanskrit:
अङ्गुष्ठमात्रः पुरुषो ज्योतिरिवाधूमकः ।
ईशानो भूतभव्यस्य स एवाद्य स उ श्वः । एतद्वै तत् ॥
verses.english: The  thumbsized  Purusha   ,   the   lord   of   the   past   and the   future,   is   like   a   flame   without   smoke;   He   stays   the same  today   and   tomorrow.   This   is   verily   That.   (Self that Nachiketa was asking about).

verses.sanskrit:
यथोदकं दुर्गे वृष्टं पर्वतेषु विधावति ।
एवं धर्मान् पृथक् पश्यंस्तानेवानुविधावति ॥
verses.english: As   rain,   fallen   on   a   hill,   streams   down   its   sides,   so   runs   he after   many   births,   who   sees     manifoldness   in   the   Self.

verses.sanskrit:
यथोदकं शुद्धे शुद्धमासिक्तं तादृगेव भवति ।
एवं मुनेर्विजानत आत्मा भवति गौतम ॥
verses.english: As   water that is  pure, when  poured   into   water that is  pure  remains  pure, so does the   Self  of the realised one  remains   pure   on   knowing   (uniting   with)   Brahman , O! Gautama !

verses.sanskrit:
पुरमेकादशद्वारमजस्यावक्रचेतसः ।
अनुष्ठाय न शोचति विमुक्तश्च विमुच्यते । एतद्वै तत् ॥
verses.english: There   is   the   city   of   eleven   gates   belonging   to   Him   who   is unborn   and   of   unchanging   consciousness.   By   meditating  upon   Him, one   grieves   no   more;   and   being   liberated   one   becomes   free   from birth   and   death.   This   is   indeed   That (Self that Nachiketa was asking about).

verses.sanskrit:
हँसः शुचिषद्वसुरन्तरिक्षसद्-
    होता वेदिषदतिथिर्दुरोणसत् ।
नृषद्वरसदृतसद्व्योमसद्
    अब्जा गोजा ऋतजा अद्रिजा ऋतं बृहत् ॥
verses.english: The Atman  is   the   Sun   in   the   heavens,   the   air   in   the   sky,   the   fire in   the   altar,   the   guest   in   the   house;   He   dwells   in   men,   in   Gods,  in   the   Sacrifice,   in   space.   He   is   all   that   is   born   in   water,   all that   is   born   on   earth,   in   the   sacrifice   and   on   the   moutains.   He is   the   True   and   the   Great.

verses.sanskrit:
ऊर्ध्वं प्राणमुन्नयत्यपानं प्रत्यगस्यति ।
मध्ये वामनमासीनं विश्वे देवा उपासते ॥
verses.english: He   it   is   that   leads   the   life-breath   upwards   and   casts   the lower   breath   downwards.   All   the   senses   minister   to   Him,   the Adorable,   who   is   seated   in   the   centre.

verses.sanskrit:
अस्य विस्रंसमानस्य शरीरस्थस्य देहिनः ।
देहाद्विमुच्यमानस्य किमत्र परिशिष्यते । एतद्वै तत् ॥
verses.english: When   the   soul   in   the   body   is   separated   and   leaves   the   body what   is   left   over   here?   This   is   indeed   That. (Self that Nachiketa was asking about).

verses.sanskrit:
न प्राणेन नापानेन मर्त्यो जीवति कश्चन ।
इतरेण तु जीवन्ति यस्मिन्नेतावुपाश्रितौ ॥
verses.english: Man   does   not   live   by   breath   alone,   but   by   Him   who   is   the power   of   breath.

verses.sanskrit:
हन्त त इदं प्रवक्ष्यामि गुह्यं ब्रह्म सनातनम् ।
यथा च मरणं प्राप्य आत्मा भवति गौतम ॥
verses.english: Listen,    I   shall   tell   you   O !  Gautama,   of   the   secret, eternal   Brahman,   and   what   happens to   the   self   after   meeting death.

verses.sanskrit:
योनिमन्ये प्रपद्यन्ते शरीरत्वाय देहिनः ।
स्थाणुमन्येऽनुसंयन्ति यथाकर्म यथाश्रुतम् ॥
verses.english: Some   souls   seek a womb   for  the sake of a physical body and yet others go to immovables, like the plant kingdom, depending upon the  activities in the past and also in consonance with the level of awakening they have reached.

verses.sanskrit:
य एष सुप्तेषु जागर्ति कामं कामं पुरुषो निर्मिमाणः ।
तदेव शुक्रं तद्ब्रह्म तदेवामृतमुच्यते ।
तस्मिँल्लोकाः श्रिताः सर्वे तदु नात्येति कश्चन । एतद्वै तत् ॥
verses.english: This  principle  Purusha   keeping  awake  when others sleep, creating     objects   of   desires ,  that which is taintless  and said to be deathless is the  Brahman.  That is the substratum  of all the worlds,  and no one can surpass it .  This , indeed  is  that. (Self that Nachiketa was asking about).

verses.sanskrit:
अग्निर्यथैको भुवनं प्रविष्टो
    रूपं रूपं प्रतिरूपो बभूव ।
एकस्तथा सर्वभूतान्तरात्मा
    रूपं रूपं प्रतिरूपो बहिश्च ॥
verses.english: As   fire,  though  one  only  ,  having   entered   the   world,   takes  the  form   of   various    objects (which it burns )  so also though  the  all permeating brahman    is one only, takes  the  form  apparently  of the various objects  it enters  and  is  also  beyond them.

verses.sanskrit:
वायुर्यथैको भुवनं प्रविष्टो
    रूपं रूपं प्रतिरूपो बभूव ।
एकस्तथा सर्वभूतान्तरात्मा
    रूपं रूपं प्रतिरूपो बहिश्च ॥
verses.english:
As  one  air,    takes   the   shape   of   every   object   it   enters,
so   the   Self  which  permeates  into everything ,   though   one,   takes   the   shape   of   every   object   in which  it  dwells.   It   also   exists   outside   them.

verses.sanskrit:
सूर्यो यथा सर्वलोकस्य चक्षुः
    न लिप्यते चाक्षुषैर्बाह्यदोषैः ।
एकस्तथा सर्वभूतान्तरात्मा
    न लिप्यते लोकदुःखेन बाह्यः ॥
verses.english: Like the sun,  which is  the   eye   of   the   whole   world   is   not  tainted by the outward defects  of  the objects  it  lights,  which are  seen  by the eye,  in  the same  manner,  this  Brahman  which  is  inherent  in all beings  is not tainted  by the sufferings  of the world,  as it is beyond  the  world.

verses.sanskrit:
एको वशी सर्वभूतान्तरात्मा
    एकं रूपं बहुधा यः करोति ।
तमात्मस्थं येऽनुपश्यन्ति धीराः
    तेषां सुखं शाश्वतं नेतरेषाम् ॥
verses.english: Eternal bliss  falls to  the  lot of those  wise ones  who realise  Him,  existing  within,  the  one controller,  the  inner being  of all beings  and being  are  manifests  in  myriad  forms  and not to the lot of others.

verses.sanskrit:
नित्योऽनित्यानां चेतनश्चेतनानाम्
    एको बहूनां यो विदधाति कामान् ।
तमात्मस्थं येऽनुपश्यन्ति धीराः
    तेषां शान्तिः  शाश्वती नेतरेषाम् ॥
verses.english: Those  wise  persons  who realise  Him,  who is within,  who  is  permanent  among  all others  which  are  impermanent ,  who  is  sentient  amongst  which  are  insentinent  and though  one fulfills  the desires  of  the  several,  they  have  lasting (inner) peace  and  none  others.

verses.sanskrit:
तदेतदिति मन्यन्तेऽनिर्देश्यं परमं सुखम् ।
कथं नु तद्विजानीयां किमु भाति विभाति वा ॥
verses.english: How shall I know that supreme, unspeakable Bliss which they realize directly as 'This'? Is it self-effulgent - does It shine dinstinctly, or does It not?

verses.sanskrit:
न तत्र सूर्यो भाति न चन्द्रतारकं
    नेमा विद्युतो भान्ति कुतोऽयमग्निः ।
तमेव भान्तमनुभाति सर्वं
    तस्य भासा सर्वमिदं विभाति ॥
verses.english: " Neither  the   sun,  the   moon , the   stars, nor   the  lightning shines here, nor to speak of this   fire.   Every other thing  shines  following That  and due to its effulgence everything is illumined.

verses.sanskrit:
ऊर्ध्वमूलोऽवाक्शाख एषोऽश्वत्थः सनातनः ।
तदेव शुक्रं तद्ब्रह्म तदेवामृतमुच्यते ।
तस्मिँल्लोकाः श्रिताः सर्वे तदु नात्येति कश्चन । एतद्वै तत् ॥
verses.english: With   its   root   above   and   branches   below   stands   this   Banyan-tree.   That   (root)   is   indeed   the   Pure,   that   is   Brahman, the   Immortal.   All   the   worlds   are   contained   in   It   and   no   one goes   beyond   It.   This   is   verily   That  (Self that Nachiketa was asking about).

verses.sanskrit:
यदिदं किं च जगत् सर्वं प्राण एजति निःसृतम् ।
महद्भयं वज्रमुद्यतं य एतद्विदुरमृतास्ते भवन्ति ॥
verses.english: The   whole   world,   whatever   there   is,   springs   from   and   moves in   Prana   (Brahman),   which   is   a   great   terror   like   an   upraised thunder-bolt.   Those   who   know   This   become   deathless.

verses.sanskrit:
भयादस्याग्निस्तपति भयात्तपति सूर्यः ।
भयादिन्द्रश्च वायुश्च मृत्युर्धावति पञ्चमः ॥
verses.english: The fire burns, the sun shines and Indra , the Wind God and the God of Death -  the fifth in row -  perform their tasks, all out of fear for Him.

verses.sanskrit:
इह चेदशकद्बोद्धुं प्राक्षरीरस्य विस्रसः ।
ततः सर्गेषु लोकेषु शरीरत्वाय कल्पते ॥
verses.english: If   one   is   not   able   to   realise (  Him )  before   his   body   falls, he becomes   liable   to   be   embodied   again   in   the   future creations.

verses.sanskrit:
यथाऽऽदर्शे तथाऽऽत्मनि यथा स्वप्ने तथा पितृलोके ।
यथाऽप्सु परीव ददृशे तथा गन्धर्वलोके
  छायातपयोरिव ब्रह्मलोके ॥
verses.english: In   one's   own   soul   Brahman   is   realised   clearly,   as   if   seen in   a   mirror.  In  the   world   of   the   ancestors  He   is   perceived  as  a dream (hazily);   in   the   world   of   angels   as   a   reflection   in   water and  in   the world   of   Brahma   as   light   distinct   from   shade.

verses.sanskrit:
इन्द्रियाणां पृथग्भावमुदयास्तमयौ च यत् ।
पृथगुत्पद्यमानानां मत्वा धीरो न शोचति ॥
verses.english: The   wise   one,   having   understood   the   individual   nature   of   the senses,   their   rising   and   setting   as   of   things   that   come   into   being separate   from   the   soul,   does   not   grieve.

verses.sanskrit:
इन्द्रियेभ्यः परं मनो मनसः सत्त्वमुत्तमम् ।
सत्त्वादधि महानात्मा महतोऽव्यक्तमुत्तमम् ॥
verses.english: Mind is greater than the senses,  the discriminating  power (Intellect)  is Greater than the mind  , the Great Brahman  is higher  than the intellect  and the unmanifested is greater than the Atman.

verses.sanskrit:
अव्यक्तात्तु परः पुरुषो व्यापकोऽलिङ्ग एव च ।
यं ज्ञात्वा मुच्यते जन्तुरमृतत्वं च गच्छति ॥
verses.english: The Purusha  is beyond  the unmanifested ,  is  all-pervading  and attributeless ,  realising That,  a creature  is liberated  and attains  to deathlessness.

verses.sanskrit:
न संदृशे तिष्ठति रूपमस्य
    न चक्षुषा पश्यति कश्चनैनम् ।
हृदा मनीषा मनसाऽभिक्लृप्तो
    य एतद्विदुरमृतास्ते भवन्ति ॥
verses.english: None   beholds   Him   with   the   eyes,   for   He   is   without   visible form. He   is revealed   in   the   heart   through   self-control   and   meditation. Those   who   know   Him   become   immortal.

verses.sanskrit:
यदा पञ्चावतिष्ठन्ते ज्ञानानि मनसा सह ।
बुद्धिश्च न विचेष्टते तामाहुः परमां गतिम् ॥
verses.english: When   the   five   senses   of   perception   stand   still   along   with the   mind,   and   when   the   intellect   does   not   move,   that   is   called the   Supreme   State.

verses.sanskrit:
तां योगमिति मन्यन्ते स्थिरामिन्द्रियधारणाम् ।
अप्रमत्तस्तदा भवति योगो हि प्रभवाप्ययौ ॥
verses.english: This   steadiness   of   the   mind   and   senses   is   what   is   known   as   Yoga. One   becomes   watchful at that time, for Yoga is subject to growth and decay.

verses.sanskrit:
नैव वाचा न मनसा प्राप्तुं शक्यो न चक्षुषा ।
अस्तीति ब्रुवतोऽन्यत्र कथं तदुपलभ्यते ॥
verses.english: The   Atman   cannot   be   attained   by   speech,   by   the   mind   or   by the   eye. How can It   be   realised   otherwise   than   from  those who   know   It as   existing?

verses.sanskrit:
अस्तीत्येवोपलब्धव्यस्तत्त्वभावेन चोभयोः ।
अस्तीत्येवोपलब्धस्य तत्त्वभावः प्रसीदति ॥
verses.english: He   is   to   be   realised   as   "existing",   and   then   that It has a transcendental nature.   His   innermost   nature   reveals   itself   to   those who   apprehend   It as   existing.

verses.sanskrit:
यदा सर्वे प्रमुच्यन्ते कामा येऽस्य हृदि श्रिताः ।
अथ मर्त्योऽमृतो भवत्यत्र ब्रह्म समश्नुते ॥
verses.english: The mortal gains  immortality  and  in  this  life  becomes  Brahman, when   all   the   yearnings  in  his  mind  are   dissolved.

verses.sanskrit:
यदा सर्वे प्रभिद्यन्ते हृदयस्येह ग्रन्थयः ।
अथ मर्त्योऽमृतो भवत्येतावद्ध्यनुशासनम् ॥
verses.english: In this body,  when all  those  knots  in  the  mind  are  cut  ,  then the  mortal  becomes  immortal ;  thus runs the  instruction (in Vedic scriptures).

verses.sanskrit:
शतं चैका च हृदयस्य नाड्य-
    स्तासां मूर्धानमभिनिःसृतैका ।
तयोर्ध्वमायन्नमृतत्वमेति
    विष्वङ्ङन्या उत्क्रमणे भवन्ति ॥
verses.english: Of the  hundred   and   one   are    nerves   of   the   heart;   one   extends towards  the   top (crown) of   the   head. Proceeding upwards through it, man attains deathlessness and the  others   lead  one  to   different   destinations (human or non-human form).

verses.sanskrit:
अङ्गुष्ठमात्रः पुरुषोऽन्तरात्मा
    सदा जनानां हृदये संनिविष्टः ।
तं स्वाच्छरीरात्प्रवृहेन्मुञ्जादिवेषीकां धैर्येण ।
तं विद्याच्छुक्रममृतं तं विद्याच्छुक्रममृतमिति ॥
verses.english: In  the  heart  of   everybeing  dwells,   the  inner soul,  of  the  dimension   of   the  human  thumb  ,  The Purusha,  whom through meditation  and persverance is like  drawing  out a thread from  the stalk.  Him one should know as pure  and  immortal. Him one should know as pure  and  immortal.

verses.sanskrit:
मृत्युप्रोक्तां नचिकेतोऽथ लब्ध्वा
    विद्यामेतां योगविधिं च कृत्स्नम् ।
ब्रह्मप्राप्तो विरजोऽभूद्विमृत्यु-
    रन्योऽप्येवं यो विदध्यात्ममेव ॥
verses.english: Instructed  by  Yama ,  in  the  knowledge, in this manner, as also  in the  technique  of  Yoga  Sastra,  Nachiketa  became  sinless  and transcended physical  death  and attained Brahman.  Also  any other  will do  so ,  on  realising the Brahman.
